title: Do Over

jokes.text: Hello, pussycat. Oh, really? A lot of ladies get right in the car after that line, you creepy piece of... Jack!
jokes.setup: Someone using a creepy pickup line
jokes.punchline: Liz's angry reaction cut off by realizing it's Jack
jokes.explanation: Classic cold open misdirection - sets up creepy stranger scenario then reveals it's Jack. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: That information is classified at least until Cheney dies. Which is going to be a long time from now. That man is mostly metal.
jokes.setup: Jack can't discuss his government job departure
jokes.punchline: Cheney being mostly metal explains his longevity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from classified info to absurd Cheney cyborg theory. Perfect Jack voice - corporate speak meets conspiracy theory.

jokes.text: Devin is the worst. It's like he doesn't even care when we should have cake for people whose birthdays are on the weekend. The Friday before. At lunch.
jokes.setup: Complaining about bad boss Devin
jokes.punchline: Biggest complaint is cake scheduling protocol
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's priorities are hilariously mundane. The specificity of 'Friday before, at lunch' sells it.

jokes.text: I got rid of all my Colin Firth movies in case they consider them erotica. That man can wear a sweater.
jokes.setup: Liz preparing for adoption evaluation
jokes.punchline: Colin Firth in sweaters as soft-core pornography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight into Liz's mind. The transition from 'erotica' to 'that man can wear a sweater' is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: You see, only thing Kathy and I need assistance with is deciding which John Mayer song to do it to.
jokes.setup: Jack offering to help with business
jokes.punchline: Devin overshares about his sex life with the boss's daughter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Devin's inappropriate oversharing. John Mayer detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Well, you know what they say about rumors, Jack. They make a 'rue' out of 'more' and 'S.'
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning rumors about Devin's performance
jokes.punchline: Devin butchers the 'assume' saying with nonsensical wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - shows Devin trying to sound clever but completely failing. The attempt at wisdom backfires perfectly.

jokes.text: Don't even think about talking to her when her soaps are on. Seriously, she will bite you.
jokes.setup: Warning Jack about Kathy
jokes.punchline: She literally bites people during soap operas
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal warning to absurd physical threat. Establishes Kathy's childlike behavior.

jokes.text: If there's one thing I learned from you, Jack, it's keep your friends close and your enemies so close that you're almost kissing.
jokes.setup: Devin learning from Jack's advice
jokes.punchline: Misquotes the saying with homoerotic twist
jokes.explanation: Another great Devin malapropism. Takes familiar saying and makes it absurdly literal and sexual.

jokes.text: I still don't know how that advertised Tokyo University.
jokes.setup: Tracy got a residual check from Japanese commercial
jokes.punchline: Commercial made no sense for university advertising
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. Implies Tracy did something completely inappropriate for a university ad.

jokes.text: But I'm gonna use this $300 to buy us all some boots for me.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he'll buy boots for everyone
jokes.punchline: The boots are actually just for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy selfishness. The misdirection is flawless - sets up generosity then reveals pure narcissism. Great economy.

jokes.text: My video game is selling through the rizznoof! Well, how far through the 'rizznoof'? Whoa, that's not slang. He has a speech impediment.
jokes.setup: Tracy using what sounds like slang
jokes.punchline: Kenneth reveals it's actually a speech impediment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Sets up expectation of Tracy slang then completely subverts it. Kenneth's matter-of-fact delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: I think I did it alone... and this check is the prizznoof. Now that one was just him being obnoxious.
jokes.setup: Tracy using 'prizznoof' after the impediment revelation
jokes.punchline: Kenneth clarifies this one is just Tracy being difficult
jokes.explanation: Great immediate callback that adds another layer. Shows Tracy exploiting the impediment excuse to be difficult.

jokes.text: The last time, it took me 22 years. But I know so much more now this time. I think I can do it in nine.
jokes.setup: Jack confident about working his way up again
jokes.punchline: Still planning to take 9 years from the mail room
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's delusional optimism. Nine years is still an absurdly long time.

jokes.text: With a little hard work, I'll be back to V.P. status before I'm 60. And if my home evaluation goes well I will be a mother by 50. We really can have it all.
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz discussing their optimistic timelines
jokes.punchline: Their 'having it all' involves being VP at 60 and mother at 50
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure showing both characters' lowered expectations while maintaining delusion of success.

jokes.text: How often do you entertain gentlemen sex guests? Oh, boy, that's uh... Once a year maybe? But I'd be open to cutting that down.
jokes.setup: Adoption evaluator asking about Liz's dating life
jokes.punchline: Liz admits to minimal dating and offers to reduce it further
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Liz's desperation leads her to offer reducing her already pathetic dating life. Great character work.

jokes.text: You ever run a webcam ring outta here, Liz? Gentlemen tell you what to do, you do it? At first with some feigned hesitation?
jokes.setup: Bev asking increasingly inappropriate questions
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific webcam prostitution scenario
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of inappropriate questions. The specificity of 'feigned hesitation' makes it funnier but less character-specific.

jokes.text: I was arrested once in Germany for public nudity. I thought it was a topless beach. It was a shipyard.
jokes.setup: Liz admitting to arrest for public nudity
jokes.punchline: Mistook industrial shipyard for beach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz story. The escalation from beach to shipyard is absurd but believable for her character. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: Could be. Or it could be my annual sex guest.
jokes.setup: Liz's phone ringing during evaluation
jokes.punchline: References her earlier admission about once-yearly dating
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the pathetic nature of her dating life. The timing during the evaluation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Aw, come on, it's Movie Party Thursday. I brought in my tape of circus accidents. Please, A lion eats a sad clown.
jokes.setup: Frank wanting to show his movie for office party
jokes.punchline: His entertainment is circus animals attacking clowns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank content - dark, specific, and childish. 'Sad clown' detail makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: Please, just be normal sauce for one day.
jokes.setup: Liz begging the writers to behave
jokes.punchline: Says 'normal sauce' instead of just 'normal'
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz linguistic quirk. The unnecessary 'sauce' addition is perfectly in character and quotable.

jokes.text: I mean, I don't do anything for Yolanda and she sends me those headless dolls.
jokes.setup: Jenna complaining about not being compensated
jokes.punchline: Compares to stalker who sends headless dolls for free
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna logic - comparing business relationships to stalker gifts. The headless dolls detail is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Can I hide this box of penis pasta in your dressing room?
jokes.setup: Pete asking Liz for a favor
jokes.punchline: Wants to hide anatomically inappropriate pasta
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist gag. The casualness of the request makes it funny but it's not deeply integrated into character or plot.

jokes.text: She touched me in my swimsuit area. It made me very uncomfortable. It's not at all erotic and fun, like when men do it to women.
jokes.setup: Jack describing sexual harassment
jokes.punchline: Ironically claims men touching women is erotic and fun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark satire of sexual harassment double standards. Jack's clueless delivery makes it funnier while highlighting the absurdity.

jokes.text: How far would I have to let her go to get my job back? Are we talking over the shirt? Frontsies, backsies? Or would I really have to give her my gift?
jokes.setup: Jack considering sexual favors for career advancement
jokes.punchline: Clinical breakdown of sexual acts, ending with euphemistic 'gift'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's desperation. The clinical terminology mixed with childish euphemisms works well.

jokes.text: I thought you said nine years? I was promoted again this morning. I'm now Director of Mail Systems.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning Jack's timeline revision
jokes.punchline: Jack got another promotion, reducing his timeline
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jack's earlier timeline. The absurd speed of mail room promotions is funny.

jokes.text: Is it so wrong that I just want to have one of these to grow up and resent me? Oh, you will.
jokes.setup: Liz expressing desire to be resented by child
jokes.punchline: Bev matter-of-factly confirms this will happen
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about parenting. Liz's twisted expectations met with Bev's casual confirmation is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I know what you're hinting at, Liz Lemon. I should get my rap career going again.
jokes.setup: Tracy understanding Liz's hint about gifts
jokes.punchline: Completely misinterprets it as encouragement for rap career
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy misdirection. Takes obvious hint and spins it into self-serving delusion about his rap career.

jokes.text: Don't dress for the job you have, dress for the job you want to have. So now, Manny... Tomorrow I show up for work dressed as a Mexican wrestler.
jokes.setup: Jack giving career advice about dressing for success
jokes.punchline: Manny wants to be a Mexican wrestler
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of standard career advice. The specificity of Mexican wrestler as career goal makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Banks, you've got to get a hold of yourself. You've got a company to run. Oh, I'm running it. I have a plan to quadruple profits by the year 2015. How are you possibly going to do that? The old-fashioned way, Jack. I'm gonna shut it down.
jokes.setup: Devin claiming to have plan to quadruple profits
jokes.punchline: Plan is to shut down the entire company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist business logic. The confidence in such an obviously insane plan is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: I'm gonna shut the whole thing down for two years. I mean, imagine how badly people will want light bulbs then.
jokes.setup: Devin explaining his shutdown plan
jokes.punchline: Believes artificial scarcity will increase demand for light bulbs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Devin logic - completely nonsensical but delivered with total confidence. The light bulb specificity adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's just 'G' now, Jack. I sold the 'E' to Samsung. They're 'Samesung' now.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning G.E. company
jokes.punchline: Devin sold a letter to Samsung, creating 'Samesung'
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist joke. The idea of selling individual letters and creating 'Samesung' is perfectly ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: Three of my nine siblings were adopted. And someday, I'm gonna find them.
jokes.setup: Kenneth sharing positive adoption story
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's lost track of his adopted siblings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth darkness hidden in cheerful delivery. The casual mention of losing siblings is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Hey, Rick. I'm Fred. Rick is the other black guy. Happens to everyone, right, Bev? Yeah, happens all the time to my black husband.
jokes.setup: Liz confusing two black employees
jokes.punchline: Bev admits to same confusion with her own husband
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation. Bev's casual admission makes an awkward situation even worse.

jokes.text: I first met Liz in 1993. She was fresh out of college and I had just broken up with O.J. Simpson. And can I just say something? Total gentleman.
jokes.setup: Jenna sharing history with Liz from 1993
jokes.punchline: Claims to have dated O.J. Simpson and calls him a gentleman
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Jenna moment. The timing (1993) and calling O.J. a gentleman is perfectly inappropriate and in character.

jokes.text: Then who were those kids you were yelling at the other day? Those were some child actors who had lied about being able to breakdance.
jokes.setup: Jenna confused about what kids Liz was yelling at
jokes.punchline: Child actors lied about breakdancing ability
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy. The specificity of breakdancing lies makes it funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: Actually, I lived with Liz while I was separated from my wife. In a platonic way. But I haven't lived there for a couple months now because I've been at an anger management treatment center 'cause I shot one of my co-workers with an arrow.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his living situation with Liz
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions shooting coworker with arrow
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane living situation to shocking violence. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I think it's a circus in the Philippines. The lion goes nuts and attacks this older clown. How did we start talking about this? Did you bring it up?
jokes.setup: Frank describing his circus accident video to adoption evaluator
jokes.punchline: Realizes how inappropriate this is mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's disturbing video. His delayed awareness of inappropriateness is well-done.

jokes.text: I'm going upstairs to doink Kathy Geiss.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his desperate plan to Liz
jokes.punchline: Uses childish euphemism 'doink' for serious situation
jokes.explanation: Good character work. Jack's use of childish euphemism while planning prostitution is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'd like to take this opportunity to thank everyone involved in making my video game the most profitable thing since the war on terror. Yes, I am provocative.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving speech to thank his colleagues
jokes.punchline: Compares video game profits to war profits, then congratulates himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy moment. The dark comparison followed by self-congratulation for being provocative is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Frank, for all your hard work, please accept this set of solid gold nunchucks. I will use these only for good.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving Frank a thank you gift
jokes.punchline: Solid gold nunchucks, Frank promises to use for good
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist gift. Frank's sincere promise to use golden weapons for good is beautifully naive.

jokes.text: Petey Pete! For letting us use the sound booth Please accept this chinchilla coat. Wow. You're gonna get so much nice-nice in that you're gonna have to grow an another ding...
jokes.setup: Tracy giving Pete an extravagant gift
jokes.punchline: Sexual comment gets cut off mid-word
jokes.explanation: Standard Tracy inappropriate comment. The cutoff saves it from being too crude but doesn't add much craft.

jokes.text: Now you look out for my girl, Liz Lemon 'Cause me and her go way back like spinal cords and car seats. That's not really an expression.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to vouch for Liz with folksy saying
jokes.punchline: Makes up nonsensical comparison, Bev corrects him
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy nonsense. The attempt at folksy wisdom with completely unrelated items is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Do you know what imperative means? Tell me, tell me! Important. It means important.
jokes.setup: Liz using word 'imperative' with Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth gets excited to learn new word like a child
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment. His childlike enthusiasm for vocabulary is sweet and in character.

jokes.text: Mr. Banks... look how many pushups I can do.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to distract Devin as instructed
jokes.punchline: Uses pushup demonstration as distraction method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic. Using physical fitness as distraction is naive and charming.

jokes.text: Paid his way through Princeton by working the day shift at that graveyard and the graveyard shift at that Days Inn.
jokes.setup: Jack recounting his hardworking background
jokes.punchline: Literal graveyard job mixed with graveyard shift wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on graveyard shift. The logistics of working both shifts shows Jack's impossible work ethic.

jokes.text: Thank God I don't have your biological need for children. That would make success impossible. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he doesn't want kids
jokes.punchline: Insults Liz while she's trying to adopt, her sarcastic thanks
jokes.explanation: Good Jack insensitivity. His complete lack of awareness about timing makes Liz's response funnier.

jokes.text: And now it hinges on how far I'm willing to go with a woman in Dora the Explorer panties that were clearly made for an obese child.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his career depending on Kathy
jokes.punchline: Kathy wears children's underwear in wrong size
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific imagery. The detail about sizing for 'obese child' adds another layer of wrongness.

jokes.text: No way, the best part of soap operas is when someone's twin interrupts a wedding. Or somebody pulls a gun at the fitness center.
jokes.setup: Liz disagreeing that soap operas are about sex
jokes.punchline: Lists absurd soap opera plot devices as the 'best parts'
jokes.explanation: Good soap opera satire. The specificity of 'fitness center' gun violence is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: She's wondering where my strawberry mouth is.
jokes.setup: Jack getting text from Kathy
jokes.punchline: References the strawberry lip gloss from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Jack's humiliation. 'Strawberry mouth' is appropriately infantilizing.

jokes.text: What? You mean the nursery! We gotta get this stuff back to the rental place by 5:00.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about the green room
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'nursery' is all rented and temporary
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that undercuts Liz's deception. The rental deadline adds urgency and absurdity.

jokes.text: Rick, what are you doing? Bitch, my name is Fred.
jokes.setup: Liz still confusing the two black employees
jokes.punchline: Fred's aggressive correction escalates the situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the name confusion. Fred's justified anger makes the callback work.

jokes.text: I work in the mailroom. Kathy, Bev and I are just friends. Kathy's the new CEO of our company. Bev, you better run. She's surprisingly strong.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the situation to Bev
jokes.punchline: Kathy physically attacks Bev, Jack warns about her strength
jokes.explanation: Good physical escalation. Kathy's unexpected strength adds another layer to her childlike behavior.

jokes.text: 'Unsatisfactory'? Are you okay, Bev? You had quite a fall there. Ah, I'm Bev. I'm here to do Liz's adoption evaluation.
jokes.setup: Liz getting bad evaluation, worried about Bev
jokes.punchline: Bev has amnesia and doesn't remember the evaluation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the 'do-over' concept. The amnesia plot device works well in this context.

jokes.text: I get a do-over. No, we have to take her to a hospital. 20 minutes. Just give me 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: Realizing Bev has amnesia
jokes.punchline: Liz wants to exploit injured woman before getting medical help
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Liz's desperation overriding basic human decency. Well-structured moral conflict.

jokes.text: I'm gonna give Kathy the full soap opera while you try to trick a lady with a head injury. We might not be the best people. But we're not the worst? Graduate students are the worst.
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz acknowledging their moral failings
jokes.punchline: Deflect by claiming graduate students are worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy moment. The escalation from acknowledging badness to deflecting onto grad students is perfect.

jokes.text: She reminds me of my own mom. Tough love, consistency, a little moustache in a certain light.
jokes.setup: Kenneth praising Liz as potential mother
jokes.punchline: Includes unflattering physical detail about mustache
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth sweetness with inappropriate honesty. The escalation from praise to insult is well-executed.

jokes.text: I thought you were at the fitness center with your twin?
jokes.setup: Liz encountering Kathy unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: References earlier soap opera plot devices
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Liz's soap opera knowledge. Using it as actual dialogue is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You know you're the only woman I've ever really loved.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to convince Kathy he's with Liz
jokes.punchline: Makes romantic declaration to Liz to deceive Kathy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Jack's desperate lie creates uncomfortable fake romantic tension.

jokes.text: I've been paranoid ever since that incident with the weather machine.
jokes.setup: Kathy explaining her paranoia
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having a weather machine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line. The casual mention of owning weather control technology is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Check this out. I'm gonna sue you! I'm going to sue the entire city of New York! I'm gonna sue you! You can't sue me, I'm already being sued. Double indemnity!
jokes.setup: Devin's meltdown after losing his job
jokes.punchline: Threatens to sue everyone, misuses legal term 'double indemnity'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Devin's incompetence. The misuse of 'double indemnity' shows his legal ignorance.

jokes.text: No, I just like seeing you in there.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Liz needs something
jokes.punchline: Liz admits she just enjoys seeing Jack back in his office
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending moment. Shows the genuine friendship beneath all the absurdity. More heartwarming than hilarious.

tmdb_overview: Worried that Liz's hectic lifestyle may be too much to adopt a child, Bev, the adoption agent, visits the set. Panicked, Liz tells everyone to act normal, but chaos soon ensues as a fight arises between Jenna and Tracy. Meanwhile, Jack returns to 30 Rock and vows to do whatever it takes to get his position back from Devin.
insight: Season 3 premiere fires 61 jokes in 42 minutes — 2.63-per-minute pace defines the reboot.
